ocr_text:
HUGHES NOT BOOMING MAINE STATE TICKET; Local Leaders Complain Because He Does Not Say More About Monday's Election. HAMMERS AT 8-HOUR BILL But Asserts That Those Who Say He Is Not Labor's Friend Are Not Telling the Truth.
C. E. Hughes, in speech at Lewiston, Me., answers charges that he is not labor's friend

HOW LA FOLLETTE WON.
How La Follette won, editorial
Article 10 -- No Title

END MAINE CANVASS CLOSE VOTE LIKELY; Republicans Claim 15,000 Plurality, Democrats 3,000 ;- Moose Hold Decision. AN AUGURY OF NOVEMBER Campaign Closes with a Bang, 292 Rallies Being Held In Last 24 Hours.
vote to be taken on 54-hour bill
Indicates Hughes's Election.

CLEAN SWEEP PUTS MAINE AGAIN IN REPUBLICAN FOLD; Almost Complete Returns Indicate About 13,000 Plurality on Governor. SENATOR JOHNSON BEATEN Democrats Also Lose Their Seat in the House, McGillicuddy Being About 500 Behind. PROGRESSIVES WENT BACK Voted in Large Numbers with Republicans ;- Prohibitionists Score with Milliken as Governor. REPUBLICAN SWEEP IN MAINE ELECTION
statement on result of Maine elections
WHY SHOULD WE CHANGE PRESIDENTS?; A Careful Weighing of the Themes of the Two Chief Parties and of Their Candidates.

ANTI-CATHOLICS GATHER.; Haines Says His 5,000,000 Men Will Elect the Next President.
C. D. Haines tells of plans for conference of anti-Catholics; aims to influence Presidential election
CORNELL 'VARSITY SCORES.; Shiverick and Hoffman Each Carry the Ball Over.

ROOSEVELT TO GIVE COLD HAND TO TAFT; Will Be No Union League Reception Buttons with T.R.-Taft-Hughes Pictures. RECONCILIATION IS OFF Colonel Disclaims Any Share in Get-Together Meeting and Protests Angrily to Willcox.
opposes Union League reception button with his picture and those of Taft and Hughes and protests to W. R. Willcox about reconciliation
SPEEDBOAT RACES TODAY.; Miss Minneapolis and Baby Marold Contenders for Continental Title.

HUGHES INDORSES ROOSEVELT ATTACK; Congratulates Him on Lewiston Speech and "Warmly Appreciates His Support." JOHNSON THANKS NOMINEE Friends See Hint of Support by the Californian ;- Hughes Charges Wastefulness in Preparedness.
C. E. Hughes in speech at Kansas City advocates firm policy to maintain just rights of Americans
Crisis in Suffrage Movement, Says Mrs. Catt; Votes-for-Women Leader Tells Why Emergency Convention of National Woman Suffrage Association Will Meet Next Week.

HOW SWEDISH NAVY GUARDS NEUTRALITY; Whole Force Alert Night and Day, but Territorial Waters Are Constantly Invaded. GERMANS CHIEF OFFENDERS Destroyers Constantly Holding Up Merchant Vessels and Taking All Their Oil.
Vigilance, under Prince William, in guarding against neutrality violations by belligerents
EASTON SQUAD OUT SEPT. 5.; Despite Epidemic, Lafayette Eleven Will Assemble at Water Gap.

SPEED BOAT BREAKS RECORD, THEN SINKS; Baby Marold Burns Up After Setting World's Mark for Five Miles at Detroit MISS MINNEAPOLIS WINS Finishes Best Heat in 6:47, within Miss Detroit in Second Place ;- 150,000 See Contest.
Miss Minneapolis wins second heat, Baby Marold sinks after breaking world's record for 5 miles

TWO MORE TOURS FOR HUGHES; Will Visit Middle West Again This Month, Also in October.
indorses Pres. Wilson and says theatrical world favors his reelection

WHITMAN, IN ALARM, APPEALS TO MOOSE; Perkins and Hotchkiss Will Try to Stop Seabury in Progressive Primary. BENNETT HITS GOVERNOR Says Kracke and Hervey, Who Run His Headquarters, Are Shirking Duty as Public Officials.
attacks Gov. Whitman and says his Govt. is not progressive but personal

Talks With Voters as a Straw.
copies of Roosevelt speech to be distributed to voters

SOUTHWEST FOR WILSON.; McLemore Says Mexican Policy Has Made Only New Mexico Doubtful.
J. E. Hedges denies that he is supporting R. Bacon

GREEN CAR LINES STILL CRIPPLED; Strikebreakers to be Used Today if Men Reject the Company's Peace Offer. HOLD JOBS UNTIL 1 P.M. Service Is Again Only 20 Per Cent. of Normal ;- Strikers Are More Bitter. GREEN CAR LINES STILL CRIPPLED
Co. will use strikebreakers if men reject amnesty offer

WHITMAN GETS ASSURANCES; Perkins and Hotchkiss Tell Him He Will Win Moose Nomination.
H. Reuterdahl's statement blaming him for Tampico incident in 1914 indorsed by Col. Roosevelt

FRASER LEADS ON PHILMONT COURSE; Sea View Pro. Has Card of 75 for Eighteen Holes in Philadelphia Open Tourney.
open tournament
Why Independent Voters Should Support Wilson; President Emeritus of Harvard Defends the Administration Against Recent Attacks Made by Republican Campaign Orators
SYRACUSE HARRIERS WIN.; Dartmouth and Pennsylvania Beaten in Race Over Hard Course.
Food Rates Soar in Philadelphia.

VATICAN TO BE IMPARTIAL.; Papal Secretary Sees Hope of Better Relations with France.
Cardinal Gasparri interviewed on re-establishment of diplomatic relations between Govt. and the Holy See

SEES MAINE SHIP REVIVAL.; Due to Wilson's Policies, Daniels Says in Bath Address.
Sec. Daniels in speech at Bath, Me., predicts revival in Me. because of accomplishments of Wilson Administration and ship purchase bill

GREECE ON VERGE OF JOINING ALLIES; Athens Dispatch of Wednesday Predicted Abandonment of Neutrality Within 48 Hours. SERBS DRIVE BACK BULGARS Operations in Macedonia Said to Have Ended in a "Staggering Defeat for Invaders."
all staff officers on leave recalled

DEALERS QUICKLY RAISE FOOD PRICES; Favorable News from Capital Comes Too Late to Prevent Rise Here and Elsewhere. OFFICIALS PREPARE TO ACT Public Markets and New Laws Discussed ;- Live Stock at New High Mark in Chicago. DEALERS QUICKLY RAISE FOOD PRICES
steps taken by city and State officials to prevent price boosting

RAILROADS READY IF STRIKE IS CALLED; Thousands of Emergency Men Hired and Many Ex-Employes Will Return. WILL ABANDON NIGHT RUNS Passengers, Milk and Mall to be Cared For First, Perishable Foodstuffs Next.

CALLS WILSON PLAN 'FRAME UP' BY LABOR; Letter in Railway Magazine Charges Gompers Staged White House Conferences FOR POLITICAL EFFECT Anonymous Writer Said to Have Predicted on Aug. 8 What Has Since Transpired.
Pres. Truesdale issues appeal to men pledging insurance if they do not strike

SWANN TAKES UP FOOD OVERCHARGES; District Attorney Will Prosecute Dealers Who Used the Strike Crisis to Put Up Prices. SUMMONS POULTRYMEN Wholesalers Must Explain Why 160 Carloads of Fowl Were Held Up ;- Prices Continue High.
Dist. Atty. Swann to investigate rise in prices of poultry

MELTZER ASSAILS AUTO-INOCULATION; Rockefeller Institute Scientist Would Forbid Its Use in Paralysis Cases. SERUM FROM HORSE BLOOD Physicians in This City See Value In Its Administration ;- Chickens Blamed for Epidemic.
sentenced

HUGHES COMES OUT AGAINST THE HYPHEN; Indorses All That Roosevelt Said in Maine and Emphasizes the Need of National Unity. DEFENDS AMERICAN RIGHTS Makes Three Addresses and Receives a Fourteen-Minute Ovation at Patriotic Coliseum Meeting. HUGHES COMES OUT AGAINST THE HYPHEN
attacked by Hughes in St. Louis address for his stand against Huerta
Athletic News of the Colleges
